meaning: adj.未加思考的,未深思的 vbl.unthink的过去式及过去分词
examples: The essential relation between language and death flashes up before us, but remains still unthought.
The best way to leave things unsaid is to leave them unthought , or if I've thought them, not to dwell on them.
And, at every turn of his instrument, the old modes took on unthought-of shapes and expressed new shades of feeling.
But, on reflection, he seems to have decided that the unthinkable is best left unthought.
